vulnerability.title: Install policycoreutils-python-utils package
vulnerability.description: The policycoreutils-python-utils package can be installed with the following command: $ sudo dnf install policycoreutils-python-utils
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then if ! rpm -q --quiet "policycoreutils-python-utils" ; then dnf install -y "policycoreutils-python-utils" fi else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: Installing the policycoreutils-python-utils package is a standard, non-disruptive action that does not affect authentication, networking, or filesystem configuration. It follows a typical STIG hardening pattern for package installation.

reasoning_messages.reasoning: We need to plan remediation: install policycoreutils-python-utils via dnf. Must use only tools. Must read_file and maybe check package presence? But to install, just run dnf command. But instruct to use review_plan first. According to developer instructions: must call review_plan before executing any commands. The user initial instruction says "You MUST use ONLY the provided tools". But also developer instructions: plan > review > apply. So we must do plan. We'll send plan_description. We'll call review_plan.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: Review output was not valid JSON. Raw: {"finding_id":"package_policycoreutils-python-utils_installed","is_optimal":false,"approve":true,"feedback":"The plan correctly identifies that the package is missing and installs it with dnf, which w...
The plan correctly identifies the need to install the package and uses correct commands. It will resolve the vulnerability when executed.
concerns: The plan assumes dnf is available; other distributions might use yum or another package manager.
It does not perform the kernel or OS platform check suggested by the original remediation script.
There is no error handling or logging, which could mask a failure to install.
